item.title: The January CPI inflation report is due out Friday morning. Here's what it's expected to show
item.description: Investors got some good news this week on the state of the labor market, and more may be on the way Friday on inflation.

item.title: Tariff revenue soars more than 300% as U.S. awaits Supreme Court decision
item.description: The U.S. government in January ran up a smaller deficit than a year ago, while tariff collections surged.

item.title: UK economy ekes out 0.1% growth in the fourth quarter
item.description: The U.K. economy grew a meager 0.1% in the fourth quarter, according to preliminary figures from the Office for National Statistics on Thursday.

item.title: The jobs picture still looks muddy, even with surprisingly strong January growth
item.description: January job gains were better than anything the U.S. economy saw in 2025 but not still enough to sound an all-clear.

item.title: U.S. payrolls rose by 130,000 in January, more than expected; unemployment down to 4.3%
item.description: Job growth was stronger than expected to start 2026, providing some relief to concerns about the state of the U.S. labor market.
